judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with concrete file/line citations; Report 2 goes further by also surfacing an undocumented pre-existing failing test and more explicitly ruling out 'separate checkout alone' as sufficient for the RESULTS_DB risk, making it marginally more rigorous overall.

report_1.evidence: Group A — pipeline/registry.py (one engineer, one PR): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... All three edit the same 11-line file on adjacent/overlapping lines. Splitting these across engineers guarantees merge conflicts for no benefit — one person should take the whole file in a single pass.

report_2.evidence:
Batch | Owner | Findings | Files
B | Engineer 2 | F-02, F-05, F-09 | pipeline/registry.py

report_1.evidence: Group A and Group C's F-08 check go first — they're small, self-contained, and don't block anyone... Group D's F-01/F-11 can run in parallel with Group B since they touch a different file

report_2.evidence: Batches A, B, C, E start together, in parallel, on day one. Each owns a completely disjoint set of files (prompts/*.txt vs registry.py vs rubric.py vs run_batch.py), so there is zero collision risk between them

report_1.evidence: F-06's threshold change is a product/scoring-policy decision, not a mechanical bug fix. ... I would not let an engineer pick new numbers unilaterally; this needs sign-off from whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy

report_2.evidence: F-06 ... has no target values — it names a direction, not a decision. ... this should be escalated as a flagged item and not block the rest of the campaign, with implementation deferred until specific thresholds (or a rule for deriving them) come back.

report_1.evidence: Awareness of tests/conftest.py's RESULTS_DB default (/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db, overridable via the RESULTS_DB env var) — ... whoever writes new tests (F-11, F-12) should check whether it needs creating or whether RESULTS_DB should be overridden to something ephemeral per engineer to avoid two people writing to the same path if they run tests at the same time.

report_2.evidence: tests/conftest.py:4 defines RESULTS_DB defaulting to the fixed absolute path /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db. ... parallel test runs across engineers' worktrees pointed at that same absolute path would collide. ... each engineer should set their own RESULTS_DB env override to a local scratch path rather than relying on the shared default.
